compiled_state.title: As a developer, design-wireframe skill is a redirect stub pointing to the ui-designer agent workflow
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the 868-line design-wireframe SKILL.md with a redirect stub (following the project-setup pattern) that directs users to the new agent-based workflow via preplanning. Remove the sub-agent guard since the skill no longer dispatches sub-agents.
**Why**: The design creation logic has moved to the dso:ui-designer agent and the review orchestration to preplanning — the skill file is now dead code that could confuse future development.
**Scope**:
- IN: Replace design-wireframe SKILL.md content with redirect stub; update skill metadata; remove sub-agent guard
- OUT: Deleting the skill directory entirely (redirect stubs are the established pattern); changes to other skills that reference design-wireframe (handled by docs story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, design-wireframe SKILL.md contains a redirect stub pointing to the ui-designer agent workflow via preplanning, following the same pattern as the project-setup redirect stub
  ← Satisfies: "The sprint → preplanning → design-wireframe nesting chain is eliminated" (no Skill to nest into)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Must follow the established redirect stub pattern (see project-setup SKILL.md)

comments.body: Consideration: [Consumer Impact] end-session/SKILL.md references /dso:design-wireframe as an escalation path when baseline changes lack design manifests. The redirect stub must provide clear guidance on the new preplanning-based workflow or update the end-session reference.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
